claim: CLI + Skills combination (Google's Agents CLI) allows end-to-end AI agent building from idea to production deployment without touching documentation
actionable: Use CLI tools with bundled skills to give coding agents all context they need; let them drive build, test, and deploy workflows
source_title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination

claim: Traditional frameworks (ADK, Pydantic AI) are still needed for production agents because they're token-efficient and faster than coding agent SDKs
actionable: Use coding agent SDKs (Anthropic) for internal agents and experimentation; use traditional frameworks (ADK, Pydantic) for production where latency and token efficiency matter
source_title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination

claim: Code execution in sandboxes (like Google ADK's built-in sandbox) is essential for production agents that execute arbitrary code safely
actionable: Never deploy agents with code execution to production without a sandboxed execution environment to prevent arbitrary code vulnerabilities
source_title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination

claim: Agent evaluation (testing with eval data) should not be skipped; it's the gate between implementation and deployment to production
actionable: Build eval workflows as part of your agent deployment pipeline; test before shipping to production
source_title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination

claim: Coding agent SDKs are less token-efficient but good for internal agents and experimentation; traditional frameworks are good for customer-facing production agents
actionable: Choose agents SDK for internal/experimental work; choose framework-based agents for production customer-facing systems
source_title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination
